    39→	// Environment variables: Note that the duration should be long enough that the backoff
    40→	// persists for some reasonable time (i.e. 120 seconds).  The typical base might be "1".

    42→	envBackoffDuration = "KUBE_CLIENT_BACKOFF_DURATION"
    43→)
    44→
    45→// Interface captures the set of operations for generically interacting with Kubernetes REST apis.
    46→type Interface interface {
    47→	GetRateLimiter() flowcontrol.RateLimiter
    48→	Verb(verb string) *Request
    49→	Post() *Request
    50→	Put() *Request
    51→	Patch(pt types.PatchType) *Request
    52→	Get() *Request
    53→	Delete() *Request
    54→	APIVersion() schema.GroupVersion
    55→}
    56→
    57→// ClientContentConfig controls how RESTClient communicates with the server.
    58→//
    59→// TODO: ContentConfig will be updated to accept a Negotiator instead of a
    60→// NegotiatedSerializer and NegotiatedSerializer will be removed.
    61→type ClientContentConfig struct {
    62→	// AcceptContentTypes specifies the types the client will accept and is optional.
    63→	// If not set, ContentType will be used to define the Accept header
    64→	AcceptContentTypes string
    65→	// ContentType specifies the wire format used to communicate with the server.
    66→	// This value will be set as the Accept header on requests made to the server if
    67→	// AcceptContentTypes is not set, and as the default content type on any object
    68→	// sent to the server. If not set, "application/json" is used.
    69→	ContentType string
    70→	// GroupVersion is the API version to talk to. Must be provided when initializing
    71→	// a RESTClient directly. When initializing a Client, will be set with the default
    72→	// code version. This is used as the default group version for VersionedParams.
    73→	GroupVersion schema.GroupVersion
    74→	// Negotiator is used for obtaining encoders and decoders for multiple
    75→	// supported media types.
    76→	Negotiator runtime.ClientNegotiator

    78→
    79→// RESTClient imposes common Kubernetes API conventions on a set of resource paths.
    80→// The baseURL is expected to point to an HTTP or HTTPS path that is the parent
    81→// of one or more resources.  The server should return a decodable API resource
    82→// object, or an api.Status object which contains information about the reason for
    83→// any failure.
    84→//
    85→// Most consumers should use client.New() to get a Kubernetes API client.
    86→type RESTClient struct {
    87→	// base is the root URL for all invocations of the client
    88→	base *url.URL
    89→	// versionedAPIPath is a path segment connecting the base URL to the resource root
    90→	versionedAPIPath string
    91→
    92→	// content describes how a RESTClient encodes and decodes responses.
    93→	content requestClientContentConfigProvider
    94→
    95→	// creates BackoffManager that is passed to requests.
    96→	createBackoffMgr func() BackoffManager
    97→
    98→	// rateLimiter is shared among all requests created by this client unless specifically
    99→	// overridden.
   100→	rateLimiter flowcontrol.RateLimiter

   102→	// warningHandler is shared among all requests created by this client.
   103→	// If not set, defaultWarningHandler is used.
   104→	warningHandler WarningHandler

   107→	Client *http.Client
   108→}
   109→
   110→// NewRESTClient creates a new RESTClient. This client performs generic REST functions
   111→// such as Get, Put, Post, and Delete on specified paths.
   112→func NewRESTClient(baseURL *url.URL, versionedAPIPath string, config ClientContentConfig, rateLimiter flowcontrol.RateLimiter, client *http.Client) (*RESTClient, error) {
   113→	base := *baseURL
   114→	if !strings.HasSuffix(base.Path, "/") {
   115→		base.Path += "/"
   116→	}
   117→	base.RawQuery = ""
   118→	base.Fragment = ""
   119→
   120→	return &RESTClient{
   121→		base:             &base,
   122→		versionedAPIPath: versionedAPIPath,
   123→		content:          requestClientContentConfigProvider{base: scrubCBORContentConfigIfDisabled(config)},
   124→		createBackoffMgr: readExpBackoffConfig,
   125→		rateLimiter:      rateLimiter,
   126→		Client:           client,
   127→	}, nil
   128→}
   129→
   130→func scrubCBORContentConfigIfDisabled(content ClientContentConfig) ClientContentConfig {
   131→	if clientfeatures.FeatureGates().Enabled(clientfeatures.ClientsAllowCBOR) {
   132→		content.Negotiator = clientNegotiatorWithCBORSequenceStreamDecoder{content.Negotiator}
   133→		return content
   134→	}
   135→
   136→	if mediatype, _, err := mime.ParseMediaType(content.ContentType); err == nil && mediatype == "application/cbor" {
   137→		content.ContentType = "application/json"
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	clauses := goautoneg.ParseAccept(content.AcceptContentTypes)
   141→	scrubbed := false
   142→	for i, clause := range clauses {
   143→		if clause.Type == "application" && clause.SubType == "cbor" {
   144→			scrubbed = true
   145→			clauses[i].SubType = "json"
   146→		}
   147→	}
   148→	if !scrubbed {
   149→		// No application/cbor in AcceptContentTypes, nothing more to do.
   150→		return content
   151→	}
   152→
   153→	parts := make([]string, 0, len(clauses))
   154→	for _, clause := range clauses {
   155→		// ParseAccept does not store the parameter "q" in Params.
   156→		params := clause.Params
   157→		if clause.Q < 1 { // omit q=1, it's the default
   158→			if params == nil {
   159→				params = make(map[string]string, 1)
   160→			}
   161→			params["q"] = strconv.FormatFloat(clause.Q, 'g', 3, 32)
   162→		}
   163→		parts = append(parts, mime.FormatMediaType(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", clause.Type, clause.SubType), params))
   164→	}
   165→	content.AcceptContentTypes = strings.Join(parts, ",")
   166→
   167→	return content
   168→}

   170→// GetRateLimiter returns rate limiter for a given client, or nil if it's called on a nil client
   171→func (c *RESTClient) GetRateLimiter() flowcontrol.RateLimiter {
   172→	if c == nil {
   173→		return nil
   174→	}
   175→	return c.rateLimiter
   176→}

   178→// readExpBackoffConfig handles the internal logic of determining what the
   179→// backoff policy is.  By default if no information is available, NoBackoff.
   180→// TODO Generalize this see #17727 .
   181→func readExpBackoffConfig() BackoffManager {
   182→	backoffBase := os.Getenv(envBackoffBase)
   183→	backoffDuration := os.Getenv(envBackoffDuration)
   184→
   185→	backoffBaseInt, errBase := strconv.ParseInt(backoffBase, 10, 64)
   186→	backoffDurationInt, errDuration := strconv.ParseInt(backoffDuration, 10, 64)
   187→	if errBase != nil || errDuration != nil {
   188→		return &NoBackoff{}
   189→	}
   190→	return &URLBackoff{
   191→		Backoff: flowcontrol.NewBackOff(
   192→			time.Duration(backoffBaseInt)*time.Second,
   193→			time.Duration(backoffDurationInt)*time.Second)}
   194→}
